title: ProductCoreDNA
description:
A self-contained slice of Operational DNA materialized for the product layer. Contains the Resources, Operations, Attributes, Signals, and Relationships transitively referenced by product.api.json and product.ui.json. Downstream technical cells read this file in place of operational.json — they never read operational DNA directly.

Product core is always DERIVED, never hand-authored. It is regenerated by the product-core-materializer whenever operational.json or the product surfaces change.

Structurally, product core flattens the operational domain hierarchy: Resources live at the top level instead of nested inside Domain.domains[].resources[]. The top-level primitives (operations, rules, outcomes, etc.) are identical in shape to their operational counterparts and reference the same sub-schemas.

domain.description: The root domain this product core belongs to. Only the identifying fields are retained — the full operational hierarchy is not copied.

path.description: The dot-separated domain path from the platform root.

resources.description: Resources referenced by product.api.json or product.ui.json, flattened from the operational domain hierarchy. Each Resource carries its Attributes and Actions inline. Includes Resources that act as Roles (referenced from Rule.allow[].role or Task.actor) — these were previously surfaced separately as `roles[]`.

operations.description: Operations surfaced by product.api.json. Each operation references a Resource+Action pair defined in resources[].

triggers.description: What initiates each surfaced operation or process — user actions, schedules, webhooks, chained operations, or signal subscriptions. Triggers with source='signal' are what the signal receiver generator reads at build time.

rules.description: Access and condition rules for surfaced operations. Cells read rules to generate auth middleware and request validators.

outcomes.description: State changes and side effects for surfaced operations, including Signals emitted via Outcome.emits. Cells read outcomes to wire signal middleware.

equations.description: Named, technology-agnostic computations referenced by surfaced operations. Implemented concretely by Scripts in Technical DNA.

signals.description: Signals emitted by surfaced operations or subscribed to by surfaced Triggers. The event-bus-cell reads this array to generate the typed publisher library and subscriber routing config.

relationships.description: Relationships between referenced Resources. Used by db-cell for foreign-key generation and by ui-cell for linked resource navigation.

domain.name: lending
domain.description: Consumer lending domain — loan origination, underwriting, and servicing.

resources.name: Loan
resources.description: A financial loan issued to a borrower with repayment terms.

signals.name: lending.Loan.Disbursed
signals.description: Published when a loan is approved and funds are disbursed to the borrower.
